title: Square of a binomial

confidence.label: Grounded
confidence.meaning: symbolically proven to follow from the previous step

steps.operation: Rewrite the square as a product of two identical binomials
steps.justification: The definition of squaring a quantity is to multiply it by itself.

confidence.label: Grounded
confidence.meaning: symbolically proven to follow from the previous step
confidence.reason: symbolically equivalent to the previous step

steps.justification: Distribute each term from the first binomial to each term in the second binomial.
steps.input_latex: a \cdot a + a \cdot b + b \cdot a + b \cdot b
steps.plain: a \cdot a + a \cdot b + b \cdot a + b \cdot b

confidence.label: Grounded
confidence.meaning: symbolically proven to follow from the previous step
confidence.reason: symbolically equivalent to the previous step

steps.justification: Simplify $a \cdot a$ to $a^2$ and $b \cdot b$ to $b^2$.
steps.input_latex: a^{2} + a \cdot b + b \cdot a + b^{2}
steps.plain: a^{2} + a \cdot b + b \cdot a + b^{2}

confidence.label: Grounded
confidence.meaning: symbolically proven to follow from the previous step
confidence.reason: symbolically equivalent to the previous step

steps.operation: Use the commutative property of multiplication and combine like terms
steps.justification: Since $b \cdot a = a \cdot b$, we can combine $a \cdot b + b \cdot a$ to $2 \cdot a \cdot b$.

confidence.label: Grounded
confidence.meaning: symbolically proven to follow from the previous step
confidence.reason: symbolically equivalent to the previous step

overall_confidence.label: Grounded
overall_confidence.meaning: symbolically proven to follow from the previous step

__power_1.name: \left(a + b\right)^{2}
__power_1.description: This term represents the square of the sum of the two variables, $a$ and $b$.

a.name: a
a.description: This variable represents the first term in the binomial $(a+b)$.

b.name: b
b.description: This variable represents the second term in the binomial $(a+b)$.

__power_4.name: a^{2}
__power_4.description: This term represents the square of the first variable, $a$.

__power_7.name: b^{2}
__power_7.description: This term represents the square of the second variable, $b$.

goal: This derivation shows how to expand the binomial $(a+b)^2$ into its trinomial form $a^2 + 2ab + b^2$, which is a fundamental identity in algebra.
followups: What is the general formula for $(a+b)^3$?
Can you show me an example of using this formula with numbers?
How does this relate to factoring a perfect square trinomial?
